ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Bak Kut Teh or lion's mane mushroom hotpot soup base. FOODON_03311737 (soup food product) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Soups and broths) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for Bak Kut Teh herbal hotpot soup base with lion's mane mushroom.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Bak Kut Teh hotpot soup base or lion's mane mushroom broth.

main_ingredients: Chinese herbs (dang gui, chuan xiong, yu zhu, dried longan)

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately 400 ml of hotpot soup base, representing a typical individual hotpot broth portion as served at a Singapore hotpot restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Mushroom soup, canned, condensed — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; adapted with reference to HPB Singapore herbal soup data and published lion's mane mushroom nutritional analyses
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for black Bak Kut Teh hotpot soup base with lion's mane mushroom. Values are estimated from: (1) USDA SR Legacy clear mushroom broth/soup data (NDB 06403, 06037), (2) HPB Singapore herbal soup reference values, and (3) published nutritional composition of Hericium erinaceus (lion's mane mushroom) per 100 g broth equivalent. The soup base is predominantly water with low macronutrient density. Sodium is elevated due to dark soy sauce and salt. Values represent the broth/liquid component only, not added hotpot ingredients.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of soups and broths — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for clear vegetable and herbal broths
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for black Bak Kut Teh hotpot soup base with lion's mane mushroom. The soup base is a low-carbohydrate herbal broth (carbohydrate_g ~1.2 per 100 g). By analogy with clear vegetable broths and herbal soups in the University of Sydney GI Database, which have negligible glycemic impact, GI is set to 1 (effectively zero) per the Veritas convention for essentially zero-GI beverages and broths. Glycemic load per 400 ml serving is 0.0 given the negligible carbohydrate content. gi_category is low per GI threshold rules (GI ≤ 55 = low).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from dark soy sauce and added salt
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this soup base due to its high sodium content (approximately 380 mg per 100 g, equating to ~1,520 mg per 400 ml serving), which represents a substantial proportion of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg; requesting a reduced-sodium version or diluting the broth is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate and negligible glycemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: This herbal soup base has a very low carbohydrate content (~1.2 g per 100 g) and negligible glycemic impact (GI effectively zero), making it suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without high-carbohydrate hotpot additions such as noodles or starchy vegetables; however, sodium intake should still be monitored.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium; low saturated fat; potential benefit from lion's mane mushroom bioactives
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While the soup base is low in saturated fat and cholesterol, its high sodium content poses a cardiovascular risk for individuals with existing heart disease; lion's mane mushroom (Hericium erinaceus) contains beta-glucans with emerging evidence for lipid-modulating effects, but clinical significance in this diluted broth form is uncertain, and sodium restriction remains the primary dietary concern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Black Bak Kut Teh with Lion's Mane Mushroom Hotpot Soup Base is a dark, richly herbal Chinese-Singaporean broth served as a hotpot soup base. Bak Kut Teh (肉骨茶, literally 'meat bone tea') is a traditional dish originating in the Hokkien and Teochew communities of Singapore and Malaysia, historically prepared by simmering pork ribs with a complex blend of Chinese medicinal herbs and spices including dang gui (angelica root), chuan xiong, yu zhu (Solomon's seal), dried longan, star anise, cinnamon, cloves, garlic, and black pepper. The 'black' variant uses dark soy sauce to produce a deeper, more intensely flavoured and coloured broth compared to the lighter Teochew-style version. This particular preparation substitutes or supplements the traditional pork with lion's mane mushroom (Hericium erinaceus, 猴头菇), a prized edible and medicinal fungus known for its delicate seafood-like flavour, meaty texture, and bioactive compounds including hericenones and erinacines, which have been studied for neuroprotective and immunomodulatory properties. The soup base is brewed over an extended period to extract maximum flavour and herbal compounds. As a hotpot soup base, it is served in a communal pot into which diners add raw ingredients such as vegetables, tofu, mushrooms, and proteins. Nutritionally, the broth is predominantly water (~96.5 g per 100 g) with very low energy (~12 kcal per 100 g), minimal protein (~0.8 g), negligible fat (~0.3 g), and low carbohydrate (~1.2 g). Sodium is notably elevated (~380 mg per 100 g) due to dark soy sauce and salt, making sodium management the primary clinical consideration. The glycemic impact is negligible. Lion's mane mushroom contributes beta-glucans and polysaccharides with emerging evidence for immune and cognitive health benefits. This dish is suitable for vegetarians and vegans in this mushroom-based formulation and is halal-friendly.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for black Bak Kut Teh hotpot soup base with lion's mane mushroom exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore, USDA, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy clear mushroom broth and herbal soup reference data, cross-referenced with published lion's mane mushroom nutritional composition. The estimation is defensible and internally consistent but cannot be classified as high confidence without an exact approved-source match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values represent the soup base broth only, not any additional hotpot ingredients added by the diner. 2. Sodium content is estimated at ~380 mg per 100 g based on dark soy sauce and salt contribution to a diluted herbal broth; actual sodium may vary significantly by restaurant preparation. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — plant-based herbal broth with no animal fat or hydrogenated oils). 4. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — this is a mushroom-based vegetarian broth with no animal-derived cholesterol). 5. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources used for this broth type. 6. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources. 7. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources. 8. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources. 9. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources. 10. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources. 11. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources. 12. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources. 13. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources. 14. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources. 15. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage/broth contains negligible glycemic carbohydrates (~1.2 g per 100 g); gi_category low per convention (GI ≤ 55 = low). Glycemic load per 400 ml serving is 0.0. 16. The food_group is retained as Beverages per the input record, consistent with the classification of hotpot soup bases as liquid food products. 17. Dietary flags reflect the mushroom-based formulation as served; if pork is added at the table, vegetarian/vegan status changes. 18. FoodOn and FoodEx2 codes are closest_parent matches; no exact ontology code exists for this specific dish.
